ocr.text: In the period from 2019 to 2024, air cargo tonnage has exhibited significant growth within the Asia-Pacific region, as evidenced
by the data for SF Express. In 2019, air cargo tonnage stood at 1,200,000 metric tons with a growth rate of 5.0% and a market
share of 15.0%. The following year, 2020, saw an increase to 1,300,000 metric tons, representing an 8.3% growth rate and a
market share of 16.0%. The trend continued into 2021 with tonnage reaching 1,400,000 metric tons, reflecting a growth rate of
7.7% and a 17.0% market share. By 2022, air cargo tonnage further expanded to 1,600,000 metric tons, achieving a robust
growth rate of 14.3%, thus increasing the market share to 18.5%. In 2023, the tonnage reached 1,800,000 metric tons with a
growth rate of 12.5% and a market share of 19.0%.
Year  AirCargoTonnage  GrowthRate  MarketShare
The projections for 2024 indicate a continued upward trajectory with expected tonnage of 2,000,000 metric tons, an 11.1%
growth rate, and a market share of 20.0%. The average air cargo tonnage over this period is calculated at 1,500,000 metric tons,
with an average growth rate of 10.0% and a market share of 17.5%. Forecasts predict a further increase to 2,200,000 metric tons
in future years, maintaining a 10.0% growth rate and a market share of 22.0%. Cumulatively, the total air cargo tonnage from
